title: St. Michael the Archangel
description: A study in negative space and architectural form. Pale ochre planes intersect against a field of warm white, exploring the tension between structure and emptiness.

title: Threshold
description: A doorway that is also a horizon. This piece began as a charcoal sketch made at dusk and evolved into something neither interior nor exterior — a liminal moment held still.

title: After Rain
description: Painted from memory rather than observation. The colours are wrong in the right way — the sky too violet, the pavement too blue — capturing feeling over fact.

title: Interior with Light
description: Morning light through venetian blinds. The room is anonymous, the light is specific. A meditation on how illumination transforms the ordinary into the remarkable.

title: Erosion I
description: The first in a series examining geological time. Sand, pigment, and resin layered and sanded back — surfaces that carry traces of their own making.

title: Field Notes
description: Compiled from small drawings made outdoors over a single afternoon. Each mark is a notation — not of landscape, but of attention.
